event_overview.answer: Hack Horizon 2.0 is a national-level 24-hour hackathon where innovation meets reality. It is scheduled for the 10th and 11th of April 2026 at the ARKA JAIN University.

organizers_and_partners.answer:
The organizing committee and official partners for the event are structured as follows:

- **Organizer:** School of Engineering & IT, ARKA JAIN University
- **In Collaboration With:** IBM and Google Developer Groups (GDG) On Campus AJU
- **In Association With:** Code & Compute Society, IT Club, and IIC.

past_success_2025.answer:
Hack Horizon 2025 featured 450+ participants and 117 teams. The top winning teams were:
- Team Helix (1st Prize)
- Team GitCoders (2nd Prize)
- Team B. Bros (3rd Prize)

eligibility.answer:
Here are the eligibility and participation criteria:

• The hackathon is open to anyone currently enrolled in a recognizable educational institution.
• Participants must register as a team of 3 to 5 members.
• Each participant can only be part of one team.

rules_and_regulations.answer:
The rules for the hackathon are as follows:
- Teams must have a unique name and a team leader.
- Participants must bring valid ID, their own laptops, and create a new GitHub repo for their project.
- Code must be pushed at intervals, and solutions must be original.
- Participants cannot leave campus until the closing ceremony, and must maintain backups.
- Late submissions are not evaluated.

registration.answer:
The registration details are as follows:
- **Fee:** Rs. 800 per team
- **Deadline:** March 25, 2026
- **Payment Instructions:** The team leader must make a single UPI payment for the entire team and ensure 'Hack Horizon 2026' is mentioned in the transaction message.

themes.answer:
The official themes for Hack Horizon 2.0 are:

• Agriculture & Food
• Governance & Civic Tech
• Cities & Infrastructure
• Community & Social Impact
• Health & Wellness
• Environment & Sustainability

timeline.answer:
The official event scheduled is organised as follows:

**Day 1 (April 10, 2026)**
• 09:00 AM: Participant Reporting
• 10:00 AM: Inaugural Session
• 11:00 AM: Hackathon Commencement

**Day 2 (April 11, 2026)**
• 10:00 AM - 11:00 AM: Project Submissions & Presentations
• 11:00 AM: Hackathon Conclusion
• 11:00 AM - 11:45 AM: Closing Ceremony

prize_pool.answer: There is a total prize pool worth of Rs 50,000.

facilities.answer:
Guests are kindly requested to note the following provisions regarding the upcoming schedule:

• Guest house accommodations on April 9th are reserved solely for the 40 out-of-state participants.
• Complimentary accommodations will be provided for all in-state participants on April 10th.
• On April 10th, comprehensive meal services will be provided for all attendees, accompanied by a scheduled fun event.
• On April 11th, catering provisions will consist of breakfast and lunch only.
• High-speed internet access will remain available to all participants throughout the duration of the event.

sponsorship_tiers.answer:
Sponsorship offers brand visibility and engagement.
 Packages include:
 Diamond Sponsor (Rs 1,00,000+) with keynotes and social media mentions
 Gold Sponsor (Rs 50,000+) with banners and print ads
and Silver Sponsor (Rs 20,000+) with poster ads

contact_info.answer:
For any inquiries or further assistance, please email hackhorizon@arkajainuniversity.ac.in or reach out to our designated team members:

• Faculty Convener: Prof. Mamatha V (+91 78588 12586)

problem_statements.answer: The problem statements will be officially disclosed on the day of the event.

certificate.answer: A Certificate of Participation will be awarded to all attendees, while top-performing teams will be presented with distinctive Winner Certificates.

chatbot_creators.answer:
The Hack Horizon 2.0 chatbot was officially developed by the following individuals:

• Adeeb Razi (Organiser, CCS)

• Samia Sayeed (Member, CCS)

judges.answer: The judges will be revealed on 11th April 2026 during the presentation.

Mentor.answer: The mentors for Hack Horizon 2.0 will be announced on the day of the event(10th April 2026) to provide guidance and support to the participants throughout the hackathon.
